file_name: GSITECHNOLOGYINC_11_16_2009-EX-10.2-INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AGREEMENT between SONY ELECTRONICS INC. and GSI TECHNOLOGY, INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 Licensed Patent(s) The term "Licensed Patent" or "Licensed Patents" means any patent or patents issued at any time in any country (including any inventor's certificates, extension, reissued, renewal and reexamined patents), which (1) is based on any invention made on or prior to the Effective Date, including any patents for inventions disclosed in patent applications filed on or prior to the Effective Date, and issuing from any of those patent applications or any patent applications which are continuations, continuing applications, continuations-in-part or divisions of those patent applications, or on any foreign counterpart of any of those patent applications, (2) (a) is owned by SONY or any of its Affiliates or (b) under which SONY or its Affiliates have the right to grant licenses without payment by SONY or any of its Affiliates of additional royalties or other consideration to a third party (excluding payments for inventions made by that third party while employed by SONY or any of its Affiliates), and (3) would, in the absence of a license granted herein, be infringed by making, using, selling, offering for sale, importing or supplying any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products). "Licensed Patents" shall exclude the Transferred Patents.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 Licensed Patent(s) The term "Licensed Patent" or "Licensed Patents" means any patent or patents issued in the United States, which (1) is based on any invention made after the Effective Date, including any patents for inventions disclosed in patent applications filed after the Effective Date, and issuing from any of those patent applications or any patent applications which are continuations, continuing applications, continuations-in-part or divisions of those patent applications, or on any foreign counterpart of any of those patent applications, (2) (a) is owned by SONY or any of its Affiliates or (b) under which SONY or its Affiliates have the right to grant licenses without payment by SONY or any of its Affiliates of additional royalties or other consideration to a third party (excluding payments for inventions made by that third party while employed by SONY or any of its Affiliates), and (3) would, in the absence of a license granted herein, be infringed by making, using, selling, offering for sale, importing or supplying any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products). "Licensed Patents" shall exclude the Transferred Patents.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified 'Licensed Patents' as patents issued at any time in any country based on inventions prior to the Effective Date. The modified text restricts the definition to patents issued only in the United States, based on inventions made after the Effective Date. This introduces a contradiction because it makes a specific geographical location to be 'United States' and the time frame to patents made 'after' Effective date. Therefore, Section 3.1, which grants rights under the Licensed Patents for products including the SRAM Products will now have a much narrower interpretation, potentially leading to confusion and disputes over the scope of the licensed rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Grant by SONY SONY, on behalf of itself and its Affiliates, hereby grants to PURCHASER a worldwide, non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free license (a) under the Licensed Patents to make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products); and (b) to use, reproduce, modify, prepare derivative works of, perform, display, and otherwise practice and exploit in any manner any and all of the SRAM Intellectual Property in connection with the use and exploitation of the 3 Transferred Assets, and to make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import Competing Products (including, without limitation, SRAM Products).
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 1.4 significantly narrows the definition of 'Licensed Patents' to only include patents issued in the United States based on inventions made after the Effective Date. This directly contradicts Section 3.1, which grants a 'worldwide' license under the 'Licensed Patents'. The geographic restriction introduced in the modified text conflicts with the 'worldwide' scope of the license granted in Section 3.1. This change is a strong contradiction because it makes the global rights originally granted significantly narrower.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 No Implied License or Other Assignment Except as expressly set out in this IP Agreement and the Asset Purchase Agreement, neither party grants to the other Party, and each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Party has not granted to it, any other license explicitly or implicitly under any Intellectual Property nor has either party made any transfer or assignment to the other Party of any Intellectual Property or intellectual property rights.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 No Implied License or Other Assignment Except as expressly set out in this IP Agreement, the Asset Purchase Agreement, and any verbal agreements made between SONY and PURCHASER, neither party grants to the other Party, and each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Party has not granted to it, any other license explicitly or implicitly under any Intellectual Property nor has either party made any transfer or assignment to the other Party of any Intellectual Property or intellectual property rights.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that no implied license or other assignment is granted except as explicitly set out in the IP Agreement and Asset Purchase Agreement. The modified text adds 'any verbal agreements made between SONY and PURCHASER' as an exception. This creates an in-text contradiction because the IP Agreement is meant to be the sole repository of the agreement on intellectual property, as stated in section 9.5. Adding 'any verbal agreements' introduces uncertainty and the potential for disputes, as verbal agreements are not documented and their terms may be unclear or contested. The scope of what constitutes a 'verbal agreement' is now subject to interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.5 Entire Agreement This IP Agreement and the Asset Purchase Agreement (including the Schedules and Exhibits hereto and thereto and the ancillary documents thereto) supersedes any other agreement, whether written or oral, that may have been made or entered into by any Party relating to the matters contemplated hereby and constitutes the entire agreement by and among the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'any verbal agreements made between SONY and PURCHASER' as an exception to the exclusivity of the IP Agreement and Asset Purchase Agreement regarding intellectual property rights. This directly contradicts section 9.5, which states that the IP Agreement and Asset Purchase Agreement supersede any other agreement, 'whether written or oral,' and constitute the entire agreement. The introduction of potentially undocumented verbal agreements introduces ambiguity and the potential for disputes, undermining the entire agreement clause.

perturbation.original_text: 8.12 Transferability All Transferred Patents will be fully transferable, alienable or licensable by PURCHASER from and after the Closing without restriction and without payment of any kind to any third party other than requirements under applicable laws to file documents with and pay fees to patent offices. There are no Liens on any of the Transferred Patents. (For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties acknowledge that SONY's licensees under Portfolio Cross Licenses already hold licenses to the Transferred Patents and do not need a license from PURCHASER to practice the Transferred Patents.)
perturbation.changed_text: 8.12 Transferability All Transferred Patents will be fully transferable, alienable or licensable by PURCHASER from and after the Closing without restriction and without payment of any kind to any third party other than requirements under applicable laws to file documents with and pay fees to patent offices, except for licenses previously granted by SONY to third parties. There are no Liens on any of the Transferred Patents. (For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties acknowledge that SONY's licensees under Portfolio Cross Licenses already hold licenses to the Transferred Patents and do not need a license from PURCHASER to practice the Transferred Patents.)
perturbation.explanation: The original text says that the transferred patents are fully transferable without restriction, aside from legal requirements to file documents with patent offices. However, the modification adds 'except for licenses previously granted by SONY to third parties'. This introduces ambiguity regarding the full rights that PURCHASER receives. While the existing exception for 'SONY's licensees under Portfolio Cross Licenses' acknowledges existing licenses, this addition creates uncertainty about any other pre-existing licenses granted by SONY and introduces an in-text contradiction, because the licences granted will require an extra step before transferring. The nature and extent of those licences now needs to be determined, which introduces uncertainty in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.12 Transferability All Transferred Patents will be fully transferable, alienable or licensable by PURCHASER from and after the Closing without restriction and without payment of any kind to any third party other than requirements under applicable laws to file documents with and pay fees to patent offices, except for licenses previously granted by SONY to third parties. There are no Liens on any of the Transferred Patents. (For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties acknowledge that SONY's licensees under Portfolio Cross Licenses already hold licenses to the Transferred Patents and do not need a license from PURCHASER to practice the Transferred Patents.)
perturbation.justification: The original text states that transferred patents will be fully transferable 'without restriction'. The modified text adds 'except for licenses previously granted by SONY to third parties'. This creates a direct contradiction because the existence of pre-existing licenses inherently restricts the purchaser's ability to fully transfer the patents. The purchaser would have to respect the rights of existing licensees, thereby limiting transferability. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as it introduces ambiguity regarding the full rights the purchaser receives and creates uncertainty.
